steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $6x^2(x + 7)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find an expression equivalent to $6x^3+42x^2$ by factoring out the greatest common factor.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^3(6 + 42x)$: distributes to $6x^3+42x^4$, not the original. ✗
  • Choice B — $6x^2(x + 36)$: distributes to $6x^3+216x^2$; the constant $36$ is wrong because $42\div6=7$, not $36$. ✗
  • Choice C — $6x^2(x + 7)$: distributes to $6x^3+42x^2$. ✓
  • Choice D — $x^2(6 + 42x)$: distributes to $6x^2+42x^3$, which swaps the exponents. ✗
That is the correct answer.
